denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/23/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100245489          Group #: GRP-1031
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-108268
Claim #: CLM-2026-189644
Date of Service (requested): 02/03/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 02/05/2026 for Spravato (esketamine) nasal therapy related to Persistent depressive disorder (F34.1). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Spravato (esketamine) nasal therapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Persistent depressive disorder (F34.1)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0131 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0131 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A less costly modality is appropriate before Spravato (esketamine) nasal therapy for Persistent depressive disorder (F34.1). Plan policy requires step-down diagnostic or therapeutic approach.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with modality substitution. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100245489) and reference PA-2026-108268. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Persistent depressive disorder (F34.1). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Spravato (esketamine) nasal therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed venlafaxine XR 225 mg and mirtazapine 30 mg; PHQ-9 ≥18 for nine months. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.
